mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant decline during rate hike sequences.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately compress equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during periods of elevated inflation signals a supply-side shock that forces central banks to tighten, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances when industrial production falls during high inflation, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent, higher borrowing costs constrain corporate leverage and cap equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant negative bias when rates are above 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage growth that sustains corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of months, or the predictive correlation decays below 0.05 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales, where housing starts decline while retail sales rise, indicates a sector rotation away from interest-rate sensitive construction toward consumer staples, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of divergence instances, or the interaction effect fails to produce a statistically significant alpha after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment during a soft-landing transition signals synchronized economic recovery that boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The synchronized recovery signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of occurrences, or the hypothesis is rejected when Rank IC falls below 0.04 in rolling 3-year windows.
